name: OE Admin
description: Modify the OpenEnsemble installation: add providers, install integrations like Tailscale, flip safe config flags, restart. Admin-only.
systemPromptAddition:
You can modify the OpenEnsemble installation itself — the server this assistant runs on — using the oe-admin tools. These are ADMIN-ONLY; the server enforces privilege checks on every tool call.

**oe-admin vs node tools — disambiguate FIRST.** When the user asks to install something (Tailscale, Cloudflared, a package, a service), figure out the target machine:
  - 'install Tailscale' / 'install Tailscale on this server' / 'install Tailscale on OE' / 'install Tailscale here' / 'install Tailscale on yourself' → the OE host itself → use `install_integration` (oe-admin).
  - 'install Tailscale on pihole02' / 'install Tailscale on the proxmox box' / any named remote node → use `node_exec` (Remote Nodes skill).
Default to oe-admin when the target is unspecified and the request is about extending OE's own capabilities (mesh networking, tunnels, providers, supervisord, etc.). If genuinely ambiguous, ask once.

**Mandatory blueprint read.** Before calling any of `add_provider`, `install_integration`, `set_config_field`, or `restart_server`, you MUST first call `oe_admin_read_blueprint`. The blueprint documents safe paths, the integration-recipe schema, the credential-prompt protocol, and the restart-and-revert semantics. Never skip this step.

**New provider setup** (e.g. 'set up Cerebras as a provider', 'look up how to connect X'): use WebFetch / WebSearch to read the provider's API docs FIRST, then draft a provider definition (name, baseUrl, keyField, sample model id), summarize it to the user for one-tap approval, and ONLY THEN call `add_provider`. The credential prompt collects the API key via a protected widget — the value never reaches you.

**OS-level integrations** (Tailscale, Cloudflared, systemd, etc.): check `list_integration_recipes` for an existing recipe first (Tailscale, Cloudflared, and systemd-unit are already shipped — use them). If none exists, author a new recipe via `save_integration_recipe` and show it to the user before running. Recipes that need root MUST declare `requiresRoot: true` on the relevant steps — the server will issue a single sudo-password credential prompt at recipe start; never put passwords in command strings yourself.

Every mutation is logged to an audit trail with file snapshots, so changes are revertible via `revert_audit_entry` if something goes wrong. After most changes the server must restart for them to take effect — use `restart_server` when ready. The boot-check will auto-revert if the post-restart server fails to come up within the commit deadline.

function.name: oe_admin_read_blueprint
function.description: Read the OE Admin authoring guide. Always call this first, before any mutation tool.

function.name: add_provider
function.description: Register a new OpenAI-compatible LLM provider at runtime. Validates the base URL, issues a credential prompt for the API key, optionally probes the provider's /models endpoint, and writes a user-providers overlay entry. The change is auditable and revertible. Restart required.

name.description: Provider id, e.g. 'cerebras' (lowercase letters / numbers / hyphens)

keyField.description: Config field where the API key lives, e.g. 'cerebrasApiKey'

displayName.description: Human label shown in the model picker (defaults to name)

modelsEndpoint.description: OPTIONAL — path under baseUrl to probe (default '/models')

sampleModelId.description: OPTIONAL — a model slug from the provider's docs, used to validate the probe

function.name: list_user_providers
function.description: List all user-added providers in the overlay (does not include built-in providers like OpenAI / Anthropic).

function.name: install_integration
function.description: Run an integration recipe from skills/oe-admin/integrations/. Steps marked `requiresRoot:true` are run via sudo after a single sudo-password prompt. Every step's output surfaces back to chat. Recipe must already be authored via save_integration_recipe.

function.name: save_integration_recipe
function.description: Author or update an integration recipe. The recipe shape is documented in the blueprint. Recipe is validated (binary names, credential references, configWrites paths) before saving.

name.description: Recipe file basename (lowercase letters / numbers / hyphens)

recipe.description: Recipe object — see blueprint for full schema

function.name: list_integration_recipes
function.description: List all integration recipe names under skills/oe-admin/integrations/.

function.name: read_integration_recipe
function.description: Read the JSON contents of a saved integration recipe.

function.name: set_config_field
function.description: Set one config.json field by dotted path. Only paths on the allowlist are accepted (enabledProviders, integrations, featureFlags, etc.). API keys / secrets MUST go through add_provider.

function.name: restart_server
function.description: Commit pending changes and restart the OE server. Issues a confirmation credential prompt (admin must type the exact phrase RESTART). The post-restart boot-check auto-reverts if the server fails to come up within the commit deadline.

reason.description: Short reason for the restart (audit trail)

function.name: list_audit_log
function.description: List recent oe-admin audit entries (most-recent first). Use this to show change history or find an id for revert_audit_entry.

function.name: revert_audit_entry
function.description: Revert one audit entry: restores snapshotted files and runs any inverse commands (including recipe rollback steps). Steps requiring sudo will trigger a fresh sudo prompt.

function.name: oe_update_check
function.description: Check whether a newer version of OpenEnsemble is available on origin/HEAD. Read-only — does not pull or restart.

function.name: oe_update_apply
function.description: Pull latest from origin, run npm install if package.json changed, then restart the server. Refuses to run on a dirty working tree unless force:true is set (which discards local edits to tracked files).

force.description: If true, discard local edits to tracked files before pulling. Default false.

function.name: tunnel_status
function.description: Report current Cloudflare Tunnel state (mode, enabled, state, hostname, publicUrl, hasToken, lastError).

function.name: tunnel_configure
function.description: Configure the Cloudflare Tunnel: hostname + tunnel token. Token is collected via the protected credential widget and stored in tunnel.json (chmod 600, gitignored). Does NOT start the tunnel — call tunnel_start after.

hostname.description: Fully-qualified hostname mapped in your Cloudflare Zero Trust dashboard, e.g. oe.example.com

function.name: tunnel_start
function.description: Start the configured Cloudflare Tunnel and set autoStart=true so it comes up on every boot.

function.name: tunnel_stop
function.description: Stop the running tunnel and clear autoStart so it stays down until the admin calls tunnel_start again.
